channel.title: Home on Project Tzib
channel.description: Recent content in Home on Project Tzib

item.title: Hello World: Welcome to Tzib Website
item.description: This guide will walk you through the entire process of setting up a new Hugo site and installing the Explore theme. We’ll cover installing Hugo, creating a site, adding the theme, and basic configuration to get you up and running quickly.

item.title: Contact Us
item.description:
Team & Collaboration

Academic Lead

  • Claudia Quintanilla

Technical Lead

  • Sergio Armando

Collaboration Model

We welcome contributions from:

  • Archaeologists
  • Software engineers
  • GIS specialists
  • Students and researchers

Contact

  • General: project-tzib@gmail.com
  • Academic: clau.quintanilla@profesor.usac.edu.gt
  • Technical: sergioarm.gpl@gmail.com

item.title: About Us
item.description:
Open/Project Tz’ib

Open/Project Tz’ib is an open initiative that integrates archaeology and engineering to develop technological solutions for field research, documentation, and knowledge dissemination. The project operates within the context of active archaeological efforts, aiming to modernize workflows through accessible, scalable, and open-source tools.

“Tz’ib,” meaning writing or record in Mayan languages, reflects our commitment to documenting, preserving, and sharing cultural heritage through technology.

The Challenge

Archaeological work in the field faces several operational and technological limitations:
